findings.msg: Card calls DNA ligase '(ATP-dependent)', but in the E. coli replication context of this lecture bacterial DNA ligase uses NAD+, not ATP.
findings.fix: E. coli DNA ligase is NAD+-dependent; ATP-dependent ligases are the eukaryotic/phage-T4 ones.

findings.msg: Card is written against a specific graded item ('HW1 Q6', 'Figure-verified: Band 1 is the upper band'), and L1-C008 cross-references it the same way — this is reverse-engineered from one assignment, not the curriculum.
findings.fix: Rewrite as a generic gel-mobility concept card with no HW1/figure references.

findings.msg: Many cards bake in a specific past clicker/exam key ('Confirmed — answer B', 'answer C', 'clicker E', e.g. L2-P010/P011/P012, L3-C034) — that only makes sense if reverse-engineered from one exam.
findings.fix: Strip the 'answer X / confirmed in lecture' tags; keep the reasoning as standalone content.

findings.msg: Below the 2-tag minimum: L1-R001, L1-R002, L1-R004, L1-R005, and L1-C012 each carry a single tag.
findings.fix: Add a second tag (e.g. L1-R005 -> dna-structure; L1-R003-style compare-contrast for the definition cards; who-what-when/foundations pairings).

findings.msg: L2-R006 and L2-R033 are near-identical 'describe E. coli oriC (size/AT-rich/13-mers/9-mers/consensus)' recall cards.
findings.fix: Merge into one; repurpose R033 to test a distinct angle (e.g. DnaA-box spacing/phasing).

findings.msg: L2-R023 and L2-R036 both ask 'what is the Hayflick limit — who/when/~divisions/cause' with the same answer.
findings.fix: Delete one or convert R036 into the senescence-vs-apoptosis nuance.

findings.msg: Reverse transcriptase was discovered in 1970 (Temin & Baltimore, independently), not 1971.
findings.fix: Change 'Overturned in 1971' to 1970.

findings.msg: The -35 and -10 elements are separated by a ~17 bp spacer (optimal 17), not '~25 bp'; this also contradicts L3-P002, which correctly says 17 bp.
findings.fix: Say separated by ~17 bp of spacer (~25 bp is the center-to-center distance, not the spacer).

findings.msg: 'Occam's razor for picking multiple-choice answers' is exam-strategy meta, not molecular-genetics content untied to the DNA/replication/transcription curriculum.
findings.fix: Remove, or replace with an actual parsimony example from genetic analysis.

findings.msg: Numerous L3 cards are keyed to one lecturer's asides ('Archbold said cross out the figure', 'she flagged as exam-important', L3-C005/C030/C034, L2-C015/C017) rather than the transferable concept.
findings.fix: Rephrase to state the biology directly; drop the 'she said' scaffolding.

findings.msg: Archaeal genome with invented bases Y/Z that 'pair only with each other' is a fabricated exam-style trick, not intro-course material.
findings.fix: Cut or demote to an optional extension card.

findings.msg: The 'Band 1 near the wells, which statement is NOT consistent' gel question is repeated near-verbatim (L1-P012, L1-P020, L1-P024, L1-C008), suggesting all trace to one exam figure.
findings.fix: Collapse to a single canonical gel-interpretation card.

findings.msg: Chargaff-violation/strandedness inference cards L1-P002 and L1-P017 omit the 'exceptions' skill tag that sibling cards L1-P015/P018/C011/C016 use for identical reasoning.
findings.fix: Add exceptions to L1-P002 and L1-P017.

findings.msg: End-replication-problem cards L2-R018 and L2-C010 omit 'directionality' though the no-upstream-3'-OH/3'-overhang logic is directional; sibling L2-R034/L2-C011 include it.
findings.fix: Add directionality to L2-R018 and L2-C010.

findings.msg: L3-P002 has the reader parse a double-stranded diagram with a caret-marked +1 but lacks figure-interpretation, which the parallel L3-P003 carries.
findings.fix: Add figure-interpretation to L3-P002.

findings.msg: Three near-identical 'Band 1 near wells / Band 2 farther — which statement is NOT consistent' problems: L1-P012, L1-P020, L1-P024.
findings.fix: Keep one; the other two add no new reasoning.

findings.msg: L3-C008 and L3-C016 both ask 'why is the -10 Pribnow box AT-rich' with the same 2-vs-3 H-bond/melting answer.
findings.fix: Collapse to one card.

findings.msg: L3-C009 and L3-C017 are the same card: 'why counting exactly 10 bp upstream to find the Pribnow box is unreliable.'
findings.fix: Remove one.

findings.msg: L2-C019 and L2-C024 both contrast topoisomerase/gyrase (supercoiling) vs ligase (nick sealing).
findings.fix: Keep the richer C024; drop C019 or refocus it.

findings.msg: L3-P001 and L3-P003 are the same exercise: derive TATAAT -10 consensus by column-wise most-frequent base from an alignment.
findings.fix: Keep one problem instance.

findings.msg: Telomerase enzyme activity was discovered/published in 1985 by Greider & Blackburn, not 1987.
findings.fix: Use 1985 for the telomerase discovery.

findings.msg: 'Topoisomerases (a.k.a. gyrases)' overstates the synonymy — DNA gyrase is one specific bacterial type-II topoisomerase, not a synonym for all topoisomerases.
findings.fix: Note gyrase is a specific type-II topoisomerase, not a general alias.

findings.msg: Thermus aquaticus was isolated/described by Brock ~1969, not 1965 (his Yellowstone sampling began mid-1960s).
findings.fix: Cite ~1969 for T. aquaticus isolation.

findings.msg: Chromosome-theory history (Wilson 1895, Sutton/Boveri 1903, Carothers 1913 grasshoppers — L1-R034/R035/R036) drifts from Unit 1's molecular scope into classical-genetics trivia.
findings.fix: Trim to the one or two names the syllabus actually names.

findings.msg: Detail like 'Brock, Yellowstone, 1965' collection of T. aquaticus is trivia beyond what the replication concept requires.
findings.fix: Keep 'thermostable, from a hot-spring bacterium'; drop the collector/date.

findings.msg: Central-dogma card L3-C005 carries an off-topic 'telomere' tag; content is about reverse transcriptase overturning the dogma, not telomere biology.
findings.fix: Drop telomere; mechanism/who-what-when already cover it.

findings.msg: DNA-ligase cards L2-R013/L2-C019 are tagged 'primer-removal', but ligase seals nicks after primer removal (Pol I does removal); the tag mislabels the enzyme's role.
findings.fix: Replace primer-removal with a more accurate grouping (leading-lagging/mechanism already present).

findings.msg: L1-R013 tags 'calculation' for pure recall of B-form dimensions with no math performed; calculation should denote a worked computation.
findings.fix: Drop calculation (dna-structure suffices); reserve calculation for the problem cards.

findings.msg: States ~10 bp/turn while L1-C024 states ~10.5 bp/turn; both are defensible (fiber vs solution) but the deck should be consistent.
findings.fix: Pick one value or note the 10 (fiber) vs 10.5 (solution) distinction.

findings.msg: Griffith/Avery/Hershey-Chase 'who-what-when' history is over-represented in L1 (~15 cards: R016-018, C002-004, C015, C017-018, C025-029, R043-045) relative to typical exam weight, while translation and mechanistic depth are comparatively thin.
findings.fix: Prune redundant history recalls; reallocate toward mechanism/application.
